response:
Sec. 1  
[THE WRITER IS CONSIDERING TWO PUBLISHERS BASED ON THEIR OFFERS FOR HER MANUSCRIPT.]  

Sec. 2  
[PUBLISHER A OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF THREE THOUSAND DOLLARS PLUS FIVE PERCENT OF THE BOOKS SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 3  
[PUBLISHER B OFFERS A FLAT FEE OF ONE THOUSAND FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS PLUS TEN PERCENT OF THE BOOKS SALES REVENUE.]  

Sec. 4  
[LET X REPRESENT THE TOTAL SALES REVENUE FROM THE BOOK.]  

Sec. 5  
[TO DETERMINE WHEN PUBLISHER A IS MORE LUCRATIVE THAN PUBLISHER B, THE INEQUALITY 3000 + 0.05X > 1500 + 0.10X SHOULD BE SOLVED.]  

Sec. 6  
[SOLVING THE INEQUALITY, WE GET 3000 - 1500 > 0.10X - 0.05X, WHICH SIMPLIFIES TO 1500 > 0.05X.]  

Sec. 7  
[DIVIDING BOTH SIDES BY 0.05 GIVES X < 30000, MEANING PUBLISHER A IS MORE BENEFICIAL WHEN SALES REVENUE IS LESS THAN THIRTY THOUSAND DOLLARS.]  

Sec. 8  
[WITHIN THE ANTICIPATED RANGE OF TEN THOUSAND TO FORTY THOUSAND DOLLARS, PUBLISHER A IS MORE BENEFICIAL FOR SALES REVENUE BETWEEN TEN THOUSAND AND THIRTY THOUSAND DOLLARS.]
